# Ember 1.2.0

Ember 1.2 focuses on making the app feel more like a serious reversing workstation: better pseudo-C output, stronger navigation in large binaries, and practical patching tools for assembly and raw bytes.

## Highlights

- Added width-aware struct field recovery in pseudo-C, so mixed-width record access is rendered as fields instead of misleading array syntax.
- Improved global declarations and pseudo-C global resolution for cleaner, more C-like output.
- Simplified more C++ standard-library names, including common `std::__cxx11::basic_string<...>` forms.
- Added unresolved indirect-call witnesses so hard call sites show why Ember could not safely name the target yet.
- Added sidebar and symbol-modal scopes for focusing on code, renamed functions, notes, bookmarks, and `.text` / executable sections.
- Improved navigation history with Alt+Left / Alt+Right, mouse back / forward buttons, breadcrumb jumps, and consistent resume state.
- Added IDA-style patch actions from the assembly view: patch assembly, patch raw bytes, and NOP an instruction.
- Made address display / rebase settings quieter with quick RVA and image-base presets.

## Toolchain And Packaging

- Release builds use GCC 16 on Linux.
- Frontend and Electron dependencies were updated.
- CFG viewer performance and CI stability were improved.

## Notes

- Assembly patching is currently x86-64 only. Other architectures can still use raw byte patches.
- Release notes are now curated from this checked-in file, so platform build jobs attach artifacts without duplicating generated changelog blocks.
